name: Ted Bundy
rationale: Confessed to 30 murders; likely many more. Executed 1989.

name: Pablo Escobar
rationale: Narco-terrorism, thousands of documented killings, bombings.

name: Charles Manson
rationale: Orchestrated the Tate-LaBianca murders through the 'Family'.

name: Al Capone
rationale: Organised crime, St. Valentine's Day Massacre, ultimately jailed for tax evasion.

name: Jeffrey Epstein
rationale: Convicted 2008 for procurement of a minor; federal sex-trafficking charges pending at his 2019 death.

name: Ghislaine Maxwell
rationale: Convicted of sex trafficking minors for Epstein's network.

name: John Gotti
rationale: Gambino crime family boss; RICO conviction including murder charges.

name: Vladimir Putin
rationale: ICC arrest warrant for alleged unlawful deportation of children from occupied Ukraine.

name: Bernie Madoff
rationale: Ran the largest Ponzi scheme in history, roughly $65B nominal.

name: Harvey Weinstein
rationale: Convicted of rape and sexual assault in multiple jurisdictions.
citation: New York (2020, 2024 retrial) · California (2022) · UK indicted

name: Willem Holleeder
rationale: Notorious Dutch organised-crime figure. Multiple convictions including complicity in several contract killings.

name: Joran van der Sloot
rationale: Dutch; convicted in Peru for the 2010 killing of Stephany Flores. Separately pleaded guilty in US for extortion.

name: Ross Ulbricht
rationale: Founded and ran Silk Road; convicted of drug distribution, money laundering, conspiracy.

name: Sam Bankman-Fried
rationale: Convicted of fraud, conspiracy, and money laundering in the FTX collapse.

name: Silvio Berlusconi
rationale: Italian PM four times; convicted in 2013 of tax fraud; multiple other trials and conflicts of interest.

name: O.J. Simpson
rationale: Acquitted criminally for the 1994 killings; civilly liable for wrongful death; later convicted of armed robbery (2008).
citation: California v. Simpson (1995) · civil (1997) · Nevada (2008) · d. 2024

name: Donald Trump
rationale: Convicted on 34 felony counts of falsifying business records. Civil findings of fraud and sexual abuse. First US president with felony convictions.
citation: People v. Trump (NY, May 2024) · Carroll civil verdicts · NY civil fraud judgment

name: Elizabeth Holmes
rationale: Theranos founder; convicted of defrauding investors.

name: Lance Armstrong
rationale: Admitted to systematic doping after years of denial; stripped of all seven Tour de France titles.
citation: USADA report (2012) · Oprah interview (2013) · civil whistleblower settlement (2018)

name: Richard Nixon
rationale: Watergate cover-up; resigned the presidency under threat of impeachment.

name: Benjamin Netanyahu
rationale: Long-serving Israeli PM; under indictment on bribery, fraud, and breach of trust charges. Separately, an ICC arrest warrant issued over alleged war crimes in Gaza.
citation: Tel Aviv District Court indictment (2019, trial ongoing) · ICC arrest warrant (Nov 2024)

name: Julius Caesar
rationale: Crossed the Rubicon under arms — textbook illegal insurrection in Roman law.

name: Michael Jackson
rationale: Acquitted on all counts in the 2005 child-molestation trial; substantial civil settlement in an earlier 1993 allegation. Complicated public record.
citation: California v. Jackson (2005 · acquitted) · 1993 civil settlement · d. 2009

name: Mata Hari
rationale: Dutch-born exotic dancer and courtesan; convicted by a French court-martial of spying for Germany in WWI and executed.

name: Hunter Biden
rationale: Convicted on federal firearms charges; pleaded guilty to federal tax charges.

name: Martha Stewart
rationale: Convicted of conspiracy, obstruction, and false statements related to an ImClone stock sale. Served five months.

name: Geert Wilders
rationale: Dutch politician; convicted of inciting discrimination over the 2014 'fewer Moroccans' chant. No fine imposed.

name: Edward Snowden
rationale: Leaked classified NSA surveillance programs; self-disclosed. Principled or criminal is the whole argument.
citation: Charged under US Espionage Act (2013) · in exile

name: Keith Richards
rationale: Decades of openly admitted heavy drug use; multiple drug arrests; no convictions serious enough to keep a Stone down.

name: Snoop Dogg
rationale: Rap career alongside open cannabis advocacy; past arrests; acquitted of 1993 murder-accessory charges.

name: Bill Clinton
rationale: Impeached by the House for perjury and obstruction relating to the Lewinsky matter. Acquitted by the Senate.

name: Banksy
rationale: Anonymous UK street artist; work is, strictly, vandalism; critically and commercially celebrated anyway.
citation: Public record of works · UK Criminal Damage Act applies

name: Nelson Mandela
rationale: Co-founded MK, sabotage campaigns against apartheid infrastructure. Later Nobel laureate, president.

name: Theo van Gogh
rationale: Dutch filmmaker and provocateur; inflammatory writing, no criminal record. Murdered in 2004.

name: Anthony Bourdain
rationale: Chef, writer, traveller; openly wrote about youthful heroin use and kitchen-industry rough edges. No criminal record.

name: Martin Luther King Jr.
rationale: ~29 arrests for civil disobedience in the service of civil rights.

name: Pim Fortuyn
rationale: Dutch politician; highly controversial platform, no criminal record. Assassinated in 2002.

name: Mahatma Gandhi
rationale: Repeated imprisonment for civil disobedience, satyagraha, tax resistance.

name: Mark Rutte
rationale: Longest-serving Dutch PM; multiple cabinet resignations (toeslagenaffaire), repeated 'functional memory' controversies, no criminal record.

name: Henry David Thoreau
rationale: Jailed one night for refusing the poll tax in protest of slavery and the Mexican–American war.

name: Rosa Parks
rationale: Arrested for refusing to give up her bus seat — deliberate, organised, catalytic.

name: Johan Cruyff
rationale: Dutch football legend. Public life largely without legal trouble.

name: Diogenes of Sinope
rationale: Lived in a barrel in the agora, insulted Alexander the Great, defaced currency. Cynic philosopher as public provocation.

name: Voltaire
rationale: Imprisoned in the Bastille twice for satire; exiled for the same.

name: Oscar Wilde
rationale: Imprisoned under laws that no longer exist; the offence was being gay in 1895.
citation: R v. Wilde (1895) · 2y hard labour · d. 1900

name: Alan Turing
rationale: Convicted of 'gross indecency' for a same-sex relationship. Pardoned posthumously.
citation: R v. Turing (1952) · Alan Turing Law (2017) · d. 1954

name: Galileo Galilei
rationale: Convicted of heresy for defending heliocentrism. House arrest for life.

name: Socrates
rationale: Convicted of impiety and corrupting youth; drank hemlock rather than compromise.

name: Albert Einstein
rationale: No documented wrongdoing of public consequence across a very examined life.

name: Mother Teresa
rationale: Albanian-Indian missionary; decades of hospice work. Historians' critiques exist; no convictions.

name: Fred Rogers
rationale: No documented wrongdoing of any consequence across a very public life.
